candidates.evidence_quote: Total revenues were $892.8 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2022 compared to $776.7 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2021
adjusted net income and adjusted net income per share for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2022 were $27.4 million and $0.56, respectively
Comparable restaurant sales at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 4.0% year-over-year in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2022
North Italia operating information: Comparable restaurant sales vs. prior year 9 %
successfully implemented incremental pricing to support our stated objective of recovering our operating margins
statements regarding sales trends and strength, returning to pre-pandemic margins, pricing actions to offset higher costs, increasing market share

We believe the strong consumer demand we experienced at our new restaurant openings and continued positive sales trends following our pricing actions demonstrates the strength and resilience of our concepts
a solid finish to a challenging year marked by persistent inflation, volatility and a dynamic operating environment
During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2022, we opened eight new restaurants, including two Cheesecake Factory restaurants in Nashville, TN and Corpus Christi, TX
Preopening costs 7,791 0.8 %
Food and beverage cost 220,469 24.7 %
Labor expenses 318,629 35.7 %
its Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share to be paid on March 21, 2023
Total principal amount of debt outstanding was $475 million, including $345 million in principal amount of 0.375% convertible senior notes due 2026
the Company had total available liquidity of $354 million, including a cash balance of $115 million and availability on its revolving credit facility of $239 million
one Cheesecake Factory restaurant opened internationally in Mexico under a licensing agreement during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2022
Represents incremental costs associated with COVID-19 such as sanitation, personal protective equipment, sick and vaccination pay, and healthcare benefits for furloughed staff members
supply chain disruptions
rising interest rates, periods of heightened inflation and market instability
increases in minimum wages and benefit costs
foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
changes in unemployment rates
adverse weather conditions in regions in which the Company’s restaurants are located
Our objectives with regard to share repurchases have been to offset the dilution to our shares outstanding that results from equity compensation grants and to supplement our earnings per share growth

During fiscal 2022, the fair value of the contingent consideration and compensation liability increased by $4.7 million to $28.6 million due to an $8.3 million increase in the fair value primarily stemming from a change in the volatility factors
In fiscal 2022, we recorded $31.4 million of expense primarily related to the impairment of three The Cheesecake Factory, one Other FRC and three Other restaurants
We recorded impairment charges related to indefinite-lived intangible assets of $180.8 million in fiscal 2020
Includes $845.1 million related to options to extend lease terms that are reasonably certain of being exercised
The timing and number of shares repurchased are also subject to legal constraints and financial covenants under our credit facility that limit share repurchases based on a defined ratio
As these developments evolve, we update our estimates, which, in turn, may result in adjustments to our effective tax rate
assumptions related to the COVID-19 pandemic, wage, product and services inflation, competitive environment, macroeconomic and industry conditions

notes: fiscal_kpis was null for this chunk (KPIs appear only on chunk_001) — no KPI rewrites.
revenue_surprise and eps_surprise follow the earnings {metric}_surprise convention for reported Q4 FY2022 results; press release compares year-over-year, no consensus figure stated in source.

supply_chain_disruption, interest_rates, minimum_wage, tariff_policy, foreign_exchange_rates, unemployment_rate, weather_conditions are grounded only in the 8-K safe-harbor risk-factor list — lower-confidence boilerplate evidence.
10-K event is part 6 of 6 (MD&A tail: buybacks, contractual obligations, FRC contingent consideration, critical accounting estimates); names coined only from this visible part.

Skipped 11 items as vague/banned/duplicative: bare macro headwinds (banned category), bakery/consumer-packaged-goods About boilerplate (no causal claim), consumer confidence and spending (near-duplicate of consumer_demand), purchase obligations, real estate obligations, standby letters of credit, interest-rate-swap termination (single past instance), incremental borrowing rate (accounting estimate), deferred tax valuation allowance (vague), demonstrations/political unrest (vague), equity-compensation dilution (mechanism folded into share_repurchase).
All 32 evidence quotes programmatically verified as exact substrings of the source event content.
